chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمِ قَتْلُهُ وَ مَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ فِيهِ الْكَفَّارَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 97 – What is allowed for the one in Ihraam to kill, and what expiation is Obligated upon him with regards to it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/328)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/328)
text: لَا بَأْسَ بِقَتْلِ الْبُرْغُوثِ وَ الْقَمْلَةِ وَ الْبَقَّةِ فِي الْحَرَمِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad, from Ibn Fazzal, from one of our companions, from Zurara,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘There is no problem with killing the flea, and the louse, and the bug inside the Harram’.
en.sarwar: 11. Ahmad has narrated from ibn Faddal from certain persons of our people from Zurarah who have said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is not harmful to kill a flea, louse or a bedbug in al- Haram (the sacred area).”’
